title: chaerefolium lamprocarpum
intro: Chaerefolium lamprocarpum, also known as the golden ragwort, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, Chaerefolium lamprocarpum has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, offering various health benefits such as immune system support and protection against oxidative stress. Chaerefolium lamprocarpum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications and allergies.
